Europe Crime Trends Interactive Comparison Map

This interactive map compares homicide, sexual assault, and theft trends across selected European countries from 2008 to 2023. Use the controls to switch between raw counts and rates per 100,000 residents, then hover over a country to compare unemployment and education context aligned to the selected year.

i About the data

The map is designed to show comparative patterns across countries, not to explain causation or measure individual risk.

Crime values can be viewed as raw counts or rates per 100,000 residents. The hover card also shows unemployment and education context.

Sources: Crime, unemployment, and education series are aligned annually from 2008–2023 for each country shown in the map. A data-only workbook can be downloaded from the controls panel.

Trends for the Selected Year

This view compares selected crime indicators and annual socioeconomic context across seven European countries for the chosen year. Raw counts tend to be highest in larger countries, while rates per 100,000 provide a more comparable country-level view.

Data sources

Crime indicators: Embedded comparison dataset for Germany, France, Italy, Greece, Norway, Lithuania, and Romania covering 2008–2023, used as the source series for this interactive map.

Unemployment overlay: World Bank WDI / ILO modeled unemployment, total percentage of labor force, annual 2008–2023.

Education overlay: EIGE/Eurostat edat_lfse_03 tertiary educational attainment, age 30–34, annual 2008–2023.

Rankings update as the crime, measure, year, or demographic overlay changes. Use rates per 100k for the most comparable country-level crime view.

Interactive comparison of selected European crime indicators and socioeconomic context, 2008–2023.
